description: fpdf is a Python port of the FPDF PHP library. Older fork of fpdf2; many examples online still use this.

documentation.example: from fpdf import FPDF; pdf = FPDF(); pdf.add_page(); pdf.output('out.pdf')
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/fpdf && python3 fpdf.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/fpdf && python3 fpdf.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/fpdf && python3 fpdf.py --help`. PDF generation (older fork of fpdf2).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the utility category. Fits when the engagement needs: PDF generation (older fork of fpdf2).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: PDF generation (older fork of fpdf2).

metadata.example: from fpdf import FPDF; pdf = FPDF(); pdf.add_page(); pdf.output('out.pdf')

name: fpdf

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
